briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 22, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Climate Change Week: Nationwide Spread of Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Today, on the occasion of the 56th Earth Day, Climate Change Week was observed across the nation, featuring a variety of events aimed at promoting carbon neutrality practices [4, 8, 10, 16, 25, 27, 31, 32, 35, 39, 47, 48]. Numerous local governments, including Gunpo, Anyang, Siheung, Cheonan, Jangseong, Boryeong, Geoje, Gimhae, and Sacheon, encouraged citizen participation through initiatives such as lights-out campaigns, environmental cleanup activities, reducing single-use items, promoting resource circulation, and carbon neutrality experience events [6, 9, 11, 15, 19, 20, 26, 33, 34, 37, 38, 40, 41, 43, 44, 76, 99, 101, 114, 115, 116, 121, 129]. Financial institutions and corporations, including KB Financial Group, Shinhan Financial Group, and Hanwha Group, also actively participated in carbon neutrality efforts by joining Earth Day lights-out campaigns and strengthening ESG management, thereby expanding daily energy-saving initiatives [5, 13, 18, 100, 117, 120, 122]. Furthermore, efforts to raise awareness and encourage citizen participation continued, with the Korea Meteorological Administration hosting a climate change design and video contest, and the Anyang City Volunteer Center promoting a carbon-neutral living lab [2, 7, 14, 22, 56, 72, 96, 105].

sections.title: 🌱 Concretizing Climate Change Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Goals
sections.content: Adaptation efforts to climate change are also being concretized. Sinan County has adapted by changing its marine cage aquaculture species in response to climate change and was selected for a pilot aquaculture support project [1, 23, 30, 46], while Jeollanam-do Province is managing forest resources, demonstrating region-specific responses [3, 42]. Meanwhile, in the Jeju region, Assemblyman Wi Seong-gon proposed wind and solar energy policies with the goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2035, emphasizing the protection of citizens' daily lives on the front lines of the climate crisis [58, 69, 73, 74, 78, 81, 85, 88, 103, 109, 113]. Additionally, Assemblyman Min Hyung-bae argued for integrating the carbon neutrality goals of Jeollanam-do and Gwangju to 2045, proposing a decisive move through changes in daily life [52, 53, 54, 84, 87, 91, 95, 107, 125, 126, 131, 135]. While local governments continue their efforts to accelerate carbon neutrality implementation, with Pyeongtaek City launching the '2050 Carbon Neutrality Green Growth Committee' and Chungcheongbuk-do Province inaugurating a Climate Crisis Response Committee [51, 60, 83, 90], the Daegu Climate Crisis Emergency Action group gave Daegu City's carbon neutrality policies a 'failing grade,' raising the need for a policy shift [89].

sections.title: ⚠️ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and its Challenges
sections.content: Globally, renewable energy generation has surpassed electricity demand for the first time ever [145], and energy transition is accelerating, with renewables displacing coal as the largest power source after 109 years [143, 164]. Domestically, efforts continue with JDC and Jeju Energy Corporation signing an agreement for renewable energy transition cooperation [137, 141, 158], and Hankook Paper expanding its wind power generation sites and strengthening new and renewable energy partnerships [138, 159, 162]. Large corporations like Naver and Kakao are also making all-out efforts to secure renewable energy, including equity investments in wind power plants to stabilize the supply for their data centers [157, 165]. However, challenges are also emerging in the process of renewable energy transition. In Germany, a paradoxical phenomenon occurred where electricity recorded 'negative prices' due to an oversupply of renewable energy [140], and domestic electricity demand is projected to increase by 7-11% by 2040 compared to previous forecasts, further increasing the need for expanded renewable energy production [146]. The UN climate chief warned that climate action could become an economic variable, citing increasing stagflationary pressures [50], and the importance of a strategic approach to ensure that the renewable energy transition does not lead to another form of 'energy dependence' was also raised [168].

sections.title: 🔴 International Society: Climate Change Impacts and Responses
sections.content: In the international community, the direct impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly prominent. Due to climate change, the hay fever season is extending by 1-2 weeks, exacerbating the suffering of patients [173, 194, 197, 198, 203], and extreme weather patterns are intensifying, with phenomena such as wildfires continuously raging fiercely even at night [186, 201]. Furthermore, a UC Berkeley Law School report revealed that climate change is accelerating migration in Central America, highlighting the severity of the climate refugee issue [181]. Environmental damages, such as increased pesticide contamination in critical ecosystems due to worsening floods, have also been reported [184]. Amidst these crises, while some, like Hegseth, dismiss climate change, the military is preparing nonetheless [172], and political attention continues, with senators questioning the Energy Secretary about climate change [187]. On Earth Day, a message was conveyed that local communities have an opportunity to lead climate action [188], and the necessity for new systems to combat climate change was also emphasized [189].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing clearly illustrates the active domestic and international efforts to address climate change, alongside the challenges we face. We must continue to build a better future through sustained attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.
